_experiment.title: Archetype plan propagation (diagnostic only)
constraints: No production renderer consumption of debug_instructional_contract
Plan values copied verbatim from DLA mixed-priority1 / mechanism-explanation fixture

activities.title: Explain temperature effects on enzyme reaction rate
activities.learner_task: Explain why reaction rate rises then falls as temperature increases within and beyond the enzyme's stable range.
activities.expected_output: A causal chain account connecting temperature change to rate outcome via intervening links.
activities.activity_preamble: You will explain the temperature–rate relationship as a transmitting process.

required_materials.purpose: Teach how temperature affects enzyme reaction rate through a causal chain.
required_materials.specification: Explain temperature→rate rise then fall with intervening links; natural prose only.

archetype_plan.start: temperature increases within and beyond the enzyme's stable range
archetype_plan.outcome: reaction rate first increases and then decreases
required_links: molecular kinetic energy and collision frequency
disruption of enzyme structure at high temperature

materials.purpose: Teach how temperature affects enzyme reaction rate through a causal chain.
materials.body: Teach how temperature affects enzyme reaction rate through a causal chain.
materials._note: Stand-in learner body = authored purpose only. No GAM-realised prose invented for this experiment.
